facts.text: The NFL was founded on September 17, 1920 in Canton, Ohio, originally called the American Professional Football Association.
The Super Bowl is the most-watched annual sporting event in the United States, regularly drawing over 100 million viewers.
Tom Brady holds the record for most Super Bowl wins by a player with 7 championships.
The Green Bay Packers are the only community-owned franchise in American professional sports, with over 360,000 shareholders.
A regulation NFL football is made of cowhide leather and weighs between 14 and 15 ounces.
The Pittsburgh Steelers and New England Patriots are tied for the most Super Bowl wins by a franchise with 6 each.
The forward pass was legalized in 1906, fundamentally changing football from a purely running game.
Jerry Rice holds the NFL career records for receptions (1,549), receiving yards (22,895), and receiving touchdowns (197).
The NFL draft was first held in 1936. The first overall pick was Jay Berwanger from the University of Chicago.
A football field is 100 yards long with two 10-yard end zones, for a total of 120 yards.
Peyton Manning held the single-season passing touchdown record with 55 in 2013, later broken by Patrick Mahomes's era of play.
The 1972 Miami Dolphins are the only team to complete a perfect season, going 17-0 including the Super Bowl.
Walter Payton, nicknamed 'Sweetness,' rushed for 16,726 career yards and held the all-time rushing record for 18 years.
The Lambeau Leap — jumping into the stands after a touchdown — was started by LeRoy Butler in 1993.
The NFL's salary cap was introduced in 1994 to promote competitive balance among teams.
Lawrence Taylor is widely considered the greatest defensive player in NFL history and revolutionized the linebacker position.
The Vince Lombardi Trophy, awarded to the Super Bowl winner, is made by Tiffany & Co. and is valued at about $50,000.
The longest field goal in NFL history is 66 yards, kicked by Justin Tucker of the Baltimore Ravens in 2021.
Jim Brown retired at age 29 while still at the peak of his career, averaging 5.2 yards per carry over 9 seasons.
The 'Immaculate Reception' by Franco Harris in 1972 is considered one of the greatest plays in NFL history.
An NFL game ball is inflated to between 12.5 and 13.5 pounds per square inch (PSI).
The Dallas Cowboys are often called 'America's Team,' a nickname coined by NFL Films in 1978.
Joe Montana went 4-0 in Super Bowls and never threw an interception in Super Bowl play.
The NFL regular season expanded from 16 games to 17 games starting in the 2021 season.
Deion Sanders is the only athlete to play in both a Super Bowl and a World Series.
The 'Ice Bowl' — the 1967 NFL Championship Game between the Packers and Cowboys — was played in -13°F (-25°C) conditions.
Emmitt Smith holds the NFL all-time rushing record with 18,355 career yards.
The two-point conversion was adopted by the NFL in 1994, having been used in college football since 1958.
Brett Favre started 297 consecutive regular-season games, one of the most remarkable iron-man streaks in NFL history.
The Chicago Bears were originally called the Decatur Staleys when they joined the league in 1920.
Patrick Mahomes became the youngest quarterback to win both an MVP award and a Super Bowl.
The NFL's overtime rules were changed in 2022 to guarantee both teams get a possession in playoff games.
Dick Butkus was so ferocious that opposing players have said they could hear him growling in the huddle.
The longest touchdown pass in NFL history is 99 yards, a feat accomplished multiple times by different quarterback-receiver duos.
Monday Night Football debuted on ABC in 1970 and became a cultural phenomenon hosted by Howard Cosell, Don Meredith, and Frank Gifford.
The 'Hail Mary' pass got its name from Roger Staubach's 50-yard touchdown throw in a 1975 playoff game.
NFL teams employ about 90 players during training camp but must cut down to a 53-man roster before the season.
The Pro Football Hall of Fame in Canton, Ohio has enshrined over 360 members since opening in 1963.
Barry Sanders retired abruptly in 1999 at age 30, just 1,457 yards short of Walter Payton's all-time rushing record.
The 'Steel Curtain' defense of the 1970s Pittsburgh Steelers is considered one of the greatest defenses in NFL history.
A quarterback's passer rating can range from 0 to 158.3 in the NFL's complex formula.
The Buffalo Bills are the only team to appear in four consecutive Super Bowls (1991-1994), losing all four.
Red Grange, 'The Galloping Ghost,' was football's first true superstar and helped legitimize professional football in the 1920s.
The NFL uses about 120 footballs per game — each team provides their own for when their offense is on the field.
The 'Purple People Eaters' was the nickname for the Minnesota Vikings' fearsome defensive line in the late 1960s and 1970s.
An NFL end zone is 10 yards deep and 53⅓ yards wide.
Drew Brees held the all-time passing yards record with 80,358 career yards before it was broken by Tom Brady.
The 'Fearsome Foursome' referred to the Los Angeles Rams' dominant defensive line in the 1960s featuring Deacon Jones and Merlin Olsen.
The NFL Combine is held annually in Indianapolis where draft prospects undergo physical and mental testing.
Reggie White, 'The Minister of Defense,' recorded 198 career sacks, second-most in NFL history.
The '12th Man' tradition at Seattle's CenturyLink Field has generated crowd noise so loud it registered on seismographs.
John Elway engineered 'The Drive' — a 98-yard touchdown march in the 1986 AFC Championship Game.
The shotgun formation was first used regularly by the San Francisco 49ers in the 1960s under coach Red Hickey.
O.J. Simpson was the first running back to rush for 2,000 yards in a single season, doing so in 1973 in just 14 games.
The 'Tuck Rule Game' in 2002 is one of the most controversial plays in NFL history, benefiting Tom Brady and the Patriots.
NFL goalposts were moved from the goal line to the back of the end zone in 1974 to reduce injuries.
Dan Marino threw for 5,084 yards and 48 touchdowns in 1984, records that stood for nearly two decades.
The 'West Coast Offense' was popularized by Bill Walsh with the San Francisco 49ers in the 1980s.
Night Train Lane set the single-season interception record with 14 picks in 1952 — his rookie year with only 12 games.
The average NFL career lasts only about 3.3 years, making it one of the shortest in professional sports.
The 'Monsters of the Midway' nickname was shared by the University of Chicago and later adopted by the Chicago Bears.
Bruce Smith holds the all-time sack record with 200 career sacks over 19 NFL seasons.
The NFL's revenue sharing model distributes television money equally among all 32 teams.
Peyton Manning is the only quarterback to win Super Bowls with two different franchises (Colts and Broncos).
The 'Helmet Catch' by David Tyree in Super Bowl XLII helped the Giants upset the undefeated Patriots.
Vince Lombardi's famous quote 'Winning isn't everything; it's the only thing' became the mantra of competitive football.
The instant replay challenge system was first introduced to the NFL in 1986, removed in 1992, and permanently reinstated in 1999.
Randy Moss set the single-season touchdown reception record with 23 in 2007 playing for the New England Patriots.
The NFL's oldest rivalry is between the Chicago Bears and Green Bay Packers, dating back to 1921.
A regulation football is 11 inches long and has a circumference of about 28 inches at the center.
The 'No-Name Defense' of the 1972 Dolphins earned their nickname because none of them were big-name stars, yet they were dominant.
Aaron Donald is widely considered the most dominant defensive player of the 2010s and 2020s, winning 3 Defensive Player of the Year awards.
The 'Philly Special' — a trick play in Super Bowl LII — saw quarterback Nick Foles catch a touchdown pass.
College football's Heisman Trophy has been awarded since 1935, named after coach John Heisman.
The NFL draft is held over three days, with rounds 1 through 7, and typically features over 250 selections.
The 'Legion of Boom' was the Seattle Seahawks' feared secondary featuring Richard Sherman, Earl Thomas, and Kam Chancellor.
Johnny Unitas held the record for consecutive games with a touchdown pass (47 games) for 52 years until Drew Brees broke it.
The 'Fail Mary' — a controversial call on a Hail Mary play during the 2012 replacement referee debacle — hastened the return of regular officials.
The first televised NFL game was on October 22, 1939, when the Brooklyn Dodgers played the Philadelphia Eagles.
LaDainian Tomlinson scored 31 touchdowns in 2006, the single-season record for total touchdowns.
The 'Greatest Show on Turf' described the high-powered St. Louis Rams offense that won Super Bowl XXXIV.
An NFL roster can carry up to 53 players on the active roster and 16 on the practice squad.
The 'Miracle at the Meadowlands' in 1978 saw a fumbled handoff lead to a game-winning return by the Eagles' Herman Edwards.
Terry Bradshaw led the Pittsburgh Steelers to four Super Bowl victories in the 1970s.
The NFL's first Black head coach in the modern era was Art Shell, hired by the Raiders in 1989.
The 'Minneapolis Miracle' — Stefon Diggs's walk-off touchdown in the 2018 NFC Divisional Playoff — is one of the greatest playoff moments ever.
NFL footballs are nicknamed 'pigskins' even though they've been made of cowhide since the 1950s.
The longest play in NFL history is a 109-yard missed field goal return by Antonio Cromartie in 2007.
Gale Sayers scored 6 touchdowns in a single game against the San Francisco 49ers in 1965.
The 'Catch' by Dwight Clark in the 1981 NFC Championship Game launched the San Francisco 49ers dynasty.
NFL referees are part-time employees and hold other full-time jobs during the offseason.
The 'Butt Fumble' by Mark Sanchez in 2012 was voted the worst play in NFL history by ESPN viewers.
mean Joe Greene's famous Coca-Cola commercial from 1979 is considered one of the greatest TV ads ever made.
The college football playoff expanded from 4 teams to 12 teams starting in the 2024-25 season.
Coach Bill Belichick has won more games than any other head coach in NFL history including postseason.
The 'Doomsday Defense' was the nickname for the Dallas Cowboys' dominant defense in the 1970s.
Adrian Peterson rushed for 296 yards against the San Diego Chargers in 2007, the single-game rushing record at the time.
The first Super Bowl in 1967 was played between the Green Bay Packers and Kansas City Chiefs, with the Packers winning 35-10.
Derrick Henry stands 6'3" and weighs 247 pounds, making him one of the largest and most powerful running backs in NFL history.
The 'Prevent Defense' is a controversial defensive alignment that many fans believe prevents teams from winning.
NFL teams are allowed 3 timeouts per half, each lasting up to 2 minutes during televised games.
Calvin Johnson, nicknamed 'Megatron,' retired at age 30 despite being one of the most physically gifted receivers ever.
The 'Terrible Towel' has been a symbol of the Pittsburgh Steelers and their fans since 1975.
The NFL expanded to 32 teams in 2002 when the Houston Texans joined the league.
Ray Lewis played 17 seasons for the Baltimore Ravens and is considered one of the greatest middle linebackers ever.
The 'Lombardi Sweep' — the Packers' famous power running play — was the signature play of Vince Lombardi's offense.
In 2007, the New England Patriots went 16-0 in the regular season but lost to the New York Giants in Super Bowl XLII.
The 'Beast Quake' run by Marshawn Lynch in the 2011 playoffs broke 9 tackles on a 67-yard touchdown run.
Travis Kelce has redefined the tight end position with his receiving ability and holds multiple postseason records for tight ends.
The first indoor Super Bowl was Super Bowl VIII in 1974, played at Rice Stadium in Houston's Astrodome area.
Deacon Jones coined the term 'sack' for tackling the quarterback behind the line of scrimmage.
The 'Music City Miracle' — a lateral-filled kickoff return by the Tennessee Titans — is one of the wildest playoff finishes ever.
NFL stadiums can seat between 61,500 (Chicago's Soldier Field) and 100,240 (MetLife Stadium for special events).
Jim Thorpe, one of the greatest athletes of the 20th century, was the first president of the NFL (then APFA) in 1920.
The 'Roughing the Passer' rule has been expanded over the years to protect quarterbacks from dangerous hits.
Tony Gonzalez retired as the all-time leader in receptions and receiving yards for tight ends.
The Wildcat formation, popularized by the 2008 Miami Dolphins, features a direct snap to a running back instead of the quarterback.
The average NFL player weighs about 245 pounds and stands 6 feet 2 inches tall.
The 'Bountygate' scandal in 2012 revealed that the New Orleans Saints had a pay-for-injury system targeting opposing players.
George Halas, 'Papa Bear,' founded the Chicago Bears and served as head coach for 40 seasons.
The 'Guarantee' by Joe Namath that the Jets would win Super Bowl III is considered one of the greatest moments in sports history.
Aaron Rodgers has the highest career passer rating in NFL history among quarterbacks with significant playing time.
The Indianapolis Colts infamously relocated from Baltimore in the middle of the night on March 29, 1984.
The NFL uses a proprietary brand of Wilson footballs, with each ball hand-crafted in Ada, Ohio.
Ed Reed holds the record for longest interception return at 107 yards.
The 'Deflategate' controversy in 2015 resulted in Tom Brady being suspended for four games.
Chuck Bednarik was the last true two-way player in the NFL, playing both center and linebacker for the Eagles through 1962.
The NFL Scouting Combine has been held in Indianapolis since 1987.
Lamar Jackson became the second unanimous NFL MVP in 2019 and won the award again in 2023.
The 'Double Doink' — a missed field goal by the Bears' Cody Parkey in the 2019 playoffs — hit both the upright and crossbar.
Don Shula holds the record for most wins as an NFL head coach with 347.
The 'Fog Bowl' in 1988 was a playoff game between the Bears and Eagles played in such thick fog that fans couldn't see the field.
Christian McCaffrey is one of only three players in NFL history to record 1,000 rushing and 1,000 receiving yards in a season.
The NFL's TV contracts are worth over $110 billion through 2033, making it the most valuable sports league in the world.
The 'Kick Six' — Chris Davis's 109-yard missed field goal return in the 2013 Iron Bowl — is one of college football's greatest plays.
Mike Ditka is the only person to win a Super Bowl as a player, assistant coach, and head coach.
The RPO (Run-Pass Option) has become one of the most popular offensive concepts in modern football.
The NFL's concussion protocol was significantly strengthened after research linked football to chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE).
Bo Jackson was named the greatest athlete of all time by ESPN, excelling in both professional football and baseball.
The 'Greatest Game Ever Played' — the 1958 NFL Championship between the Colts and Giants — went to sudden-death overtime for the first time.

Each NFL team plays 6 games against division rivals, 4 against another division in their conference, 4 against a division from the other conference, 2 interconference games based on prior year's finish, and 1 game against a cross-conference opponent with a matching record.
J.J. Watt won 3 Defensive Player of the Year awards in his first 5 full seasons, dominating like few defensive players before him.
The 'Polamalu Rule' and other rules were inspired by Troy Polamalu's unique and aggressive style of play at safety.
The NFL uses Hawk-Eye technology and multiple camera angles for replay reviews on scoring plays and turnovers.
Joe Burrow led LSU to an undefeated season and national championship in 2019, throwing 60 touchdown passes.
The longest overtime game in NFL history was a playoff game between the Dolphins and Chiefs on Christmas Day 2021, lasting 82 minutes and 8 seconds.
The 'Rooney Rule' requires NFL teams to interview minority candidates for head coaching and senior front office positions.
Tyreek Hill has been clocked at 23.24 mph while carrying the football, making him one of the fastest players in NFL history.
The NFL's first Black quarterback to start a Super Bowl was Doug Williams, who won MVP in Super Bowl XXII with 340 passing yards.
The huddle was reportedly invented by Paul Hubbard, a deaf quarterback at Gallaudet University, so opposing teams couldn't read his sign language.
Jason Kelce's speech on the steps of the Philadelphia Museum of Art after winning Super Bowl LII became an iconic moment.
A touchback on a kickoff places the ball at the 25-yard line as of a 2016 rule change, previously set at the 20.
The NFL expanded its playoff field from 12 to 14 teams starting with the 2020 season, adding one wild card per conference.
Russell Wilson was the shortest quarterback to win a Super Bowl in the modern era at 5'11".
The Washington Commanders have changed their name three times in franchise history.
The 'Madden Curse' refers to the superstition that players featured on the cover of the Madden NFL video game will suffer injuries or poor seasons.
George Blanda played 26 NFL seasons, from 1949 to 1975, the longest career in league history.
The 'Elway Cross' was the iconic helicopter play in Super Bowl XXXII where John Elway dove headfirst for a first down.
College football's Bowl Championship Series (BCS) ran from 1998 to 2013 before being replaced by the College Football Playoff.
Richard Sherman's postgame interview after the 2014 NFC Championship Game became one of the most viral moments in sports media.
The NFL uses over 700 game officials throughout the season, all of whom must pass annual fitness tests.
Davante Adams set the Packers single-season receiving touchdown record in 2020 with 18 touchdowns.
The first Super Bowl halftime show to feature a major pop act was Super Bowl XXVII in 1993 with Michael Jackson.
The 'Annexation of Puerto Rico' in the movie Little Giants is based on a real trick play called the fumblerooski.
Cooper Kupp won the receiving Triple Crown in 2021, leading the NFL in receptions, receiving yards, and receiving touchdowns.
The NFL's franchise tag allows teams to retain a player for one year at a salary based on the top players at their position.
CJ Stroud became one of the most successful rookie quarterbacks in NFL history, leading the Texans to a playoff win in 2024.
The 'Heisman Pose' — the stiff-arm stance — is based on the statue that adorns the Heisman Trophy.
The NFL has held regular-season games in London since 2007 as part of its international series.
Eric Dickerson rushed for 2,105 yards in 1984, setting the single-season rushing record that still stands.
Bobby Wagner has been selected to the Pro Bowl in nearly every season of his career, cementing his status as an all-time great linebacker.
The NFL's Thursday Night Football package moved to Amazon Prime Video exclusively starting in 2022.
The 'Philly Special' play in Super Bowl LII was originally called 'Philly Philly' in the Eagles' playbook.
A standard NFL season runs from early September through early February, spanning approximately 5 months.
The 'Blooper Bowl' — Super Bowl LIII between the Patriots and Rams — was the lowest-scoring Super Bowl ever at 13-3.
Odell Beckham Jr.'s one-handed touchdown catch against the Cowboys in 2014 is considered one of the greatest catches in NFL history.
The average cost of a 30-second Super Bowl commercial has grown from $42,000 in 1967 to over $7 million in recent years.
The 'Dome Patrol' was the nickname for the New Orleans Saints' linebacker corps in the early 1990s.
Patrick Mahomes led the Chiefs to back-to-back Super Bowl victories in 2023 and 2024.
Flag football was added to the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics, marking the first time football will be an Olympic sport.
The 'Play 60' initiative encourages kids to be active for 60 minutes daily and is the NFL's signature youth health campaign.
Peyton Manning's 'Omaha' audible call at the line of scrimmage became so famous that the city of Omaha embraced it as a marketing slogan.
Walter Camp is known as the 'Father of American Football' for establishing the line of scrimmage, the snap, and the downs system.
The NFL's youngest franchise is the Houston Texans, who began play in 2002.
Josh Allen became known for his ability to hurdle defenders, an unusual skill for a 6'5" quarterback.
The black stripe on the nose of many NFL players is called eye black and is used to reduce glare from sunlight and stadium lights.
Devin Hester holds the record for most return touchdowns (punt and kickoff combined) in NFL history with 20.
The average NFL game lasts about 3 hours and 12 minutes, but the ball is actually in play for only about 11 minutes.
Super Bowl rings are custom-designed for each winning team and typically feature diamonds and the team's logo.
The NFL's 'Taunting' penalty was emphasized in 2021, drawing criticism from players and fans for being too strict.
Saquon Barkley rushed for over 2,000 yards in 2024, joining an elite group of running backs to reach that milestone.
The NFL uses GPS tracking chips in players' shoulder pads to collect data on speed, distance, and acceleration.
The 'Punt, Pass & Kick' competition has been an NFL youth program since 1961.
Jalen Hurts became only the sixth quarterback in NFL history to rush for 10+ touchdowns and throw for 20+ in the same season.
The concept of 'garbage time' statistics refers to stats accumulated when the outcome of a game is already decided.
The 'Bus' was Jerome Bettis's nickname, and he retired after winning Super Bowl XL in his hometown of Detroit.
NFL players consume an average of 4,000 to 6,000 calories per day during the season to maintain their weight.
The longest winning streak in NFL history is 23 consecutive wins by the Indianapolis Colts from 2008 to 2009, including postseason.
Derrick Brooks, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers linebacker, was known for his community service as much as his on-field play.
The first NFL game played outside of North America was in Mexico City in 2005.
John Madden's coaching record of .731 winning percentage is the highest in NFL history among coaches with 100+ games.
The Wonderlic test is a 12-minute, 50-question cognitive ability test given to NFL draft prospects at the Combine.
Nick Foles holds the record for most touchdown passes in a single game with 7, tied with several other quarterbacks.
The NFL's 'franchise quarterback' concept drives teams to invest enormous resources in finding and keeping an elite passer.
Mike Singletary was famous for his intense pregame stare, which teammates said could be felt through walls.
The average NFL player's career earnings are approximately $3.4 million over their career.
The 'Bluegrass Miracle' in 2002 saw LSU score on a 74-yard Hail Mary to beat Kentucky, one of college football's great finishes.
Since 2002, every NFL team has been placed in one of 8 divisions with 4 teams each.
Cam Newton's Superman touchdown celebration became one of the most iconic celebrations in modern NFL history.
The pigskin got its name because early footballs were made using inflated pig bladders wrapped in leather.
Von Miller has been one of the most dominant pass rushers of his era, winning Super Bowl MVPs with two different franchises.
The 'Pooch Punt' is a short, high punt designed to be difficult to return, often used inside the opponent's 40-yard line.
The NFL's parity is intentional — the worst teams draft first to help competitive balance across the league.
Justin Jefferson set the record for receiving yards in a player's first two NFL seasons with 3,016 yards.
The 'Coach's Challenge' flag is red, and each coach gets 2 challenges per game (3 if both previous challenges are successful).
An NFL football is referred to as 'The Duke' in honor of Wellington Mara, whose childhood nickname was Duke.
The 'Hog Mollies' is a term linemen use to describe themselves, embracing the unglamorous nature of their position.
Saturday college football and Sunday NFL games have been a staple of American weekends since the mid-20th century.
George Kittle and Rob Gronkowski popularized the modern era of the athletic, pass-catching tight end.
The 2-minute warning in NFL games is a stoppage of play with 2 minutes remaining in each half, originally used when the stadium clock was unreliable.
Tailgating — the tradition of cooking and socializing in stadium parking lots before games — is a uniquely American football tradition.
The NFL has retired no league-wide jersey numbers, though individual teams have retired numbers honoring franchise legends.
Fantasy football was invented in 1962 by Wilfred 'Bill' Winkenbach, a part-owner of the Oakland Raiders.
The 'Spider 2 Y Banana' play became famous after Jon Gruden repeatedly referenced it on his QB Camp show.
Tom Brady was selected 199th overall in the 2000 NFL Draft, making him the greatest late-round pick in history.
The 'Terrible Towel' was created by Myron Cope, the legendary Pittsburgh Steelers broadcaster, in 1975.
NFL helmets now feature position-specific designs — quarterbacks have extra face protection while linemen have smaller facemasks for better vision.
Kliff Kingsbury became the first coach to be fired and rehired in the same NFL season when he returned as an offensive coordinator.
The NFL's total revenue exceeded $18 billion in 2023, making it the highest-grossing sports league in the world.
Jerome Bettis weighed 255 pounds and carried the football with a bruising, physical style that earned him the nickname 'The Bus.'
Myles Garrett set the Cleveland Browns' single-season sack record and has been one of the most feared pass rushers of the 2020s.
The 'Griddy' celebration dance was popularized in the NFL by Justin Jefferson and became a cultural phenomenon.
Arena football, played on a smaller indoor field, was founded in 1987 as an alternative to the NFL offseason.
Each NFL team has a practice squad of up to 16 players who can be elevated to the active roster on game days.
Brian Urlacher anchored the Chicago Bears defense for 13 seasons and was inducted into the Hall of Fame in 2018.
The 'Oklahoma Drill' — a one-on-one blocking/tackling drill — has been banned by many teams and leagues due to injury concerns.
The NFL's concussion and injury report is required to be published by every team on Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday during the season.
Sean McVay became the youngest head coach to win a Super Bowl at age 36 when the Rams won Super Bowl LVI.
The 'Four Verticals' play is one of the most common deep passing concepts used at every level of football.
Micah Parsons has been one of the most versatile defenders in the NFL, playing both linebacker and defensive end.
The first college football game was played on November 6, 1869 between Rutgers and Princeton.
The 'Nickel' defense gets its name from the fifth defensive back, with 'nickel' referring to the coin.
Chad Johnson legally changed his last name to 'Ochocinco' (Spanish for 85) to match his jersey number.
The 'Pistol Formation' was invented by Chris Ault at the University of Nevada and has been widely adopted in the NFL.
The NFL Pro Bowl was replaced by the Pro Bowl Games in 2023, featuring skills competitions instead of a traditional game.
Offensive linemen are typically the largest players on the field, averaging 6'5" and 315 pounds in the modern NFL.
The 'Coffin Corner' punt aims for the sideline inside the opponent's 10-yard line, pinning them deep in their own territory.
T.J. Watt tied Michael Strahan's single-season sack record with 22.5 sacks in the 2021 season.
The NFL Network launched on November 4, 2003 as a 24/7 channel dedicated to professional football.
Punters and kickers often have the longest NFL careers because their positions are less physically demanding.
The 'Tampa 2' defense was popularized by Tony Dungy and Monte Kiffin for the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the late 1990s.
NFL players undergo an average of 2.8 concussion evaluations per career, according to league injury data.
The 'Sunday Scaries' originally referred to the dread of Monday morning but now also means anxiety about your fantasy football lineup.
Fred Warner has been one of the best coverage linebackers in the NFL, helping transform the position in the modern game.
College football's 'Conference Realignment' has dramatically reshaped the landscape, with schools like USC and Texas changing conferences.
The NFL salary cap for the 2024 season was set at $255.4 million per team.
Chris Jones of the Kansas City Chiefs has been one of the most dominant interior defensive linemen of the 2020s.
The 'Dime' defense features six defensive backs and is used in obvious passing situations.
The 'Flexbone' offense, used primarily in college football, features three running backs and is a triple-option system.
Quarterback sneaks on 4th-and-1 or 3rd-and-1 have a success rate of over 80% in the NFL.
The Super Bowl's official name uses Roman numerals, a tradition that started with Super Bowl V in 1971.
Tailgate grills, folding chairs, and team flags are essential equipment for the pre-game parking lot experience.
The center is the only offensive lineman who touches the ball on every play, snapping it to the quarterback.
The 'Personal Protector' in punt formation is the player who stands closest to the punter and serves as the last line of defense.
In the NFL, a 'franchise quarterback' can earn over $50 million per year on a modern contract extension.
DeMarcus Ware recorded 138.5 sacks in his career, making him one of the most prolific pass rushers in NFL history.
The 'Snap Count' — how the quarterback signals the center to snap the ball — is a critical part of pre-snap communication.
Super Bowl Sunday is the second-largest day for food consumption in the United States after Thanksgiving.
The XFL has attempted to compete with the NFL multiple times, launching in 2001, 2020, and again in 2023 under new ownership.
Chris Godwin, Mike Evans, and the Tampa Bay Buccaneers receiving corps helped Tom Brady transition to a new team seamlessly.
The 'Intentional Grounding' penalty occurs when a quarterback throws the ball away without a nearby eligible receiver to avoid a sack.
Nick Chubb has been one of the most efficient runners in the NFL, consistently averaging over 5 yards per carry.
The 'Stick Route' is a 5-yard hitch route that quarterbacks use as a quick, reliable passing option.
The average age of an NFL rookie is 22 years old, with most players entering the draft after 3 years of college.
The 'Spot of the Foul' determines where a penalty is enforced, and it varies depending on the type of infraction.
Tua Tagovailoa's injury history sparked league-wide discussions about player safety and return-to-play protocols.
Brock Purdy, 'Mr. Irrelevant' as the last pick of the 2022 draft, became a successful starting quarterback for the 49ers.
The 'Two-Gap' technique requires a defensive lineman to control the offensive lineman and both gaps on either side of them.
NFL cheerleaders were first introduced by the Baltimore Colts in the 1960s and are now featured by most NFL teams.
Pro football's first night game was played on November 21, 1902 — years before most stadiums had electric lighting.
The 'Illegal Formation' penalty occurs when the offense doesn't have exactly 7 players on the line of scrimmage at the snap.
Nick Saban won seven national championships — six at Alabama and one at LSU — making him the most successful college football coach of the modern era.
Bear Bryant won 323 games over his coaching career at Maryland, Kentucky, Texas A&M, and Alabama, earning him the nickname 'The Bear.'
The Rose Bowl, first played in 1902, is known as 'The Granddaddy of Them All' and is the oldest bowl game in college football.
Archie Griffin of Ohio State is the only player to win the Heisman Trophy twice, taking the award in 1974 and 1975.
The 1971 'Game of the Century' between Nebraska and Oklahoma ended 35-31 and is considered one of the greatest college football games ever played.
AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas — home of the Dallas Cowboys — cost $1.2 billion to build and features the world's largest column-free interior.
SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California is the most expensive stadium ever built at approximately $5.5 billion and hosts both the Rams and Chargers.
Allegiant Stadium in Las Vegas, home of the Raiders, was the first fully enclosed stadium with a translucent roof and natural grass field.
Soldier Field in Chicago, opened in 1924, is the oldest stadium still in use in the NFL.
The Oakland Raiders moved to Los Angeles in 1982, returned to Oakland in 1995, and relocated again to Las Vegas in 2020.
The St. Louis Rams relocated to Los Angeles in 2016 after spending 21 seasons in St. Louis, sparking a massive lawsuit from the city.
The Cleveland Browns relocated to become the Baltimore Ravens in 1996, but the NFL allowed Cleveland to keep the Browns name and history for a future expansion team.
The Houston Oilers became the Tennessee Oilers in 1997 and then the Tennessee Titans in 1999 after relocating from Houston.
The original Baltimore Colts franchise moved to Indianapolis overnight in 1984, with moving vans leaving under cover of darkness to avoid legal injunctions.
The Canadian Football League uses a 110-yard field with 20-yard end zones, three downs instead of four, and 12 players per side.
The CFL's Grey Cup championship game has been played since 1909, making it older than the NFL itself.
Doug Flutie became a CFL legend with the Calgary Stampeders and Toronto Argonauts before returning to success in the NFL.
Warren Moon played six seasons in the CFL with the Edmonton Eskimos (now Elks) before his Hall of Fame NFL career, winning five consecutive Grey Cups.
The USFL (United States Football League) played spring football in 1983-1985 and famously featured future NFL stars like Steve Young, Jim Kelly, and Reggie White.
Tom Landry coached the Dallas Cowboys for 29 consecutive seasons from 1960 to 1988, compiling a 250-162-6 record.
Knute Rockne coached Notre Dame to a .881 winning percentage, the highest in college football history, before his death in a 1931 plane crash.
Bear Bryant's trademark houndstooth hat became one of the most iconic symbols in college football history.
Bobby Bowden guided Florida State to 14 consecutive top-5 finishes from 1987 to 2000, an unprecedented run of dominance.
The 'Four Horsemen' of Notre Dame — a legendary 1924 backfield — were immortalized by sportswriter Grantland Rice.
Herschel Walker won the 1982 Heisman Trophy at Georgia and later signed with the USFL's New Jersey Generals before his NFL career.
Tim Tebow won the Heisman Trophy in 2007 as a sophomore at Florida, becoming the first sophomore to win the award.
The Heisman Trophy weighs 25 pounds, stands 13.5 inches tall, and is cast in bronze.
Ricky Williams won the 1998 Heisman Trophy at Texas and set the NCAA's all-time rushing record with 6,279 career yards at the time.
Charles Woodson won the 1997 Heisman Trophy as a cornerback at Michigan, one of the rare defensive players to claim the award.
Johnny Manziel, 'Johnny Football,' won the 2012 Heisman as the first freshman to earn the trophy but struggled in his NFL career with the Browns.
Ryan Leaf, drafted second overall in 1998, is widely considered the biggest quarterback bust in NFL Draft history after a brief, tumultuous career.
JaMarcus Russell was selected first overall by the Raiders in 2007 and was out of the league by 2009, becoming one of the NFL's biggest draft busts.
Tom Brady's famous NFL Combine photo — showing him shirtless and unathletic — is often cited as proof that physical appearance doesn't predict NFL success.
The 2000 NFL Draft is considered one of the best ever, producing Tom Brady, Brian Urlacher, Jamal Lewis, and multiple Hall of Famers.
Shannon Sharpe was drafted in the seventh round in 1990 and went on to become one of the greatest tight ends in NFL history.
Terrell Davis rushed for 2,008 yards in 1998 and won Super Bowl MVP, but knee injuries cut his career short after just seven seasons.
Anthony Muñoz, a 13-time Pro Bowl offensive tackle for the Cincinnati Bengals, is widely regarded as the greatest offensive lineman in NFL history.
Jonathan Ogden was the first-ever draft pick by the Baltimore Ravens in 1996 and became one of the best left tackles the game has ever seen.
The 'Hogs' were the Washington Redskins' dominant offensive line in the 1980s, anchoring a running game that won three Super Bowls.
Larry Allen of the Dallas Cowboys could bench press 700 pounds and is considered one of the strongest players in NFL history.
Bruce Matthews played 19 seasons for the Houston Oilers/Tennessee Titans and started at every position on the offensive line during his career.
Joe Thomas played 10,363 consecutive offensive snaps for the Cleveland Browns, never missing a snap from 2007 until a torn triceps ended his career in 2017.
John Hannah of the New England Patriots was named the best offensive lineman of all time by Sports Illustrated.
Gene Upshaw played his entire 15-year career as a guard for the Oakland Raiders and later served as head of the NFL Players Association.
Randall McDaniel was a 12-time Pro Bowl guard for the Minnesota Vikings and one of the most athletic interior linemen ever.
The zone blocking scheme, popularized by Alex Gibbs with the Denver Broncos in the 1990s, emphasizes lateral movement and cut blocks.
The 46 Defense, created by Buddy Ryan, stacked eight defenders near the line of scrimmage and was used to devastating effect by the 1985 Chicago Bears.
The Cover 3 defense divides the deep field into thirds, with three defensive backs each responsible for one zone.
Bill Parcells, known as 'The Big Tuna,' coached four different NFL teams to the playoffs and won two Super Bowls with the Giants.
The 'Air Coryell' offense, developed by Don Coryell with the San Diego Chargers in the late 1970s, pioneered the modern vertical passing game.
The spread offense, which spaces receivers across the field to create one-on-one matchups, migrated from college football to the NFL in the 2000s.
Leather football helmets were standard equipment until the 1950s, when hard plastic shells gradually replaced them.
The face mask was not standard on football helmets until the late 1950s, and single-bar face masks were common into the 1960s.
Modern NFL helmets contain impact sensors and padding systems like Vicis's ZERO1 technology designed to reduce concussion risk.
Football shoulder pads have shrunk significantly since the 1980s, with modern pads being lighter and more form-fitting for better mobility.
Early football cleats were high-top leather boots with metal studs; modern cleats are lightweight, low-cut, and feature molded plastic or rubber studs.
The yellow first-down line seen on TV broadcasts was first introduced by Sportvision in 1998 and is generated by sophisticated computer graphics.
Lambeau Field in Green Bay has a heated underground turf system to prevent the field from freezing, though the 'Frozen Tundra' nickname persists.
Michigan Stadium, known as 'The Big House,' holds over 107,000 fans and is the largest stadium in the United States.
Beaver Stadium at Penn State holds over 106,000 fans and is known for its 'Whiteout' games where all fans wear white.
Neyland Stadium at the University of Tennessee holds over 100,000 fans and is famous for 'Rocky Top' playing after every score.
Dante Hall earned the nickname 'The Human Joystick' for his electrifying punt and kick returns with the Kansas City Chiefs in the early 2000s.
Billy 'White Shoes' Johnson is credited with popularizing end zone celebrations after scoring on kick returns in the 1970s.
Adam Vinatieri is the NFL's all-time leading scorer among kickers and is famous for his clutch Super Bowl-winning field goals with the Patriots.
Morten Andersen and Gary Anderson are the only kickers inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame, reflecting the position's historical underappreciation.
The NFL's 2024 kickoff rule moved the kicking team to the receiving team's 40-yard line and receiving team blockers to their own 30-35, dramatically changing return dynamics.
Steve Tasker of the Buffalo Bills is considered the greatest special teams player in NFL history, earning seven Pro Bowl selections as a gunner.
The fake punt is one of football's highest-risk, highest-reward plays, and a failed fake can shift momentum for the entire game.
The onside kick success rate has dropped below 10% in recent seasons due to rule changes that limit running starts by the kicking team.
The 'AFL-NFL Merger' in 1970 combined the two rival leagues into one and created the AFC and NFC conference structure still used today.
The NFL's 'Black Monday' refers to the day after the regular season ends, when multiple head coaches are traditionally fired.
Vince Lombardi's Green Bay Packers won five NFL championships in seven years during the 1960s, including the first two Super Bowls.
The Philadelphia Eagles won three consecutive NFL championships from 1948 to 1950 before the Super Bowl era began.
The 2001 New England Patriots, led by an unknown Tom Brady after Drew Bledsoe's injury, won the Super Bowl as 14-point underdogs.
The 1985 Chicago Bears recorded the 'Super Bowl Shuffle' rap song during the season and went on to dominate Super Bowl XX by a score of 46-10.
Tiger Stadium in Baton Rouge, Louisiana — home of the LSU Tigers — is nicknamed 'Death Valley' for its deafening crowd noise at night games.
The 'Dotting the I' tradition at Ohio State sees a sousaphone player march to the top of the Script Ohio formation to complete the letter I.
Notre Dame's 'Touchdown Jesus' mural on the Hesburgh Library is visible from inside Notre Dame Stadium and depicts Christ with raised arms.
The 'War Eagle' battle cry at Auburn University comes from a legend about a Civil War veteran's pet eagle circling the field during a game.
The 'Sooner Schooner' is the University of Oklahoma's mascot — a Conestoga wagon pulled by ponies that races onto the field after scores.
The 'Tomahawk Chop' chant at Florida State games was adopted in the late 1980s and remains one of college football's most recognizable traditions.
Green Bay's Lambeau Field parking lots host some of the NFL's most dedicated tailgaters, with brats and cheese curds as staple menu items.
Kansas City's Arrowhead Stadium held the Guinness World Record for loudest crowd roar at a sports stadium, reaching 142.2 decibels in 2014.
The 'Dawg Pound' at Cleveland's stadium is the bleacher section where Browns fans dress as dogs and bark at opposing teams.
The 'Black Hole' was the infamous section at the Oakland Coliseum where Raiders fans dressed in costume and intimidated visiting teams.
The first NFL game played in Germany was held in Munich in 2022, part of the league's expanded international series.
The 2006 Rose Bowl between Texas and USC is often called the greatest college football game of all time, with Vince Young leading a comeback victory.
The 'Kick Six' play in the 2013 Iron Bowl — where Auburn returned a missed field goal 109 yards — ended one of college football's fiercest rivalries in stunning fashion.
The 'Statue of Liberty' play helped Boise State upset Oklahoma in the 2007 Fiesta Bowl, one of the biggest upsets in bowl game history.
The 'Fifth Down' game in 1990 saw Colorado awarded an extra down due to a referee error, and they scored the winning touchdown on the illegal play.
The 'App State Upset' in 2007, when Appalachian State beat fifth-ranked Michigan, is considered the biggest upset in college football history.
Bear Bryant's final game was the 1982 Liberty Bowl; he coached Alabama to a win over Illinois and passed away just 28 days later.
The 'Iron Bowl' between Alabama and Auburn is one of the most intense rivalries in all of sports, with no neutral-site games until 1989.
Dabo Swinney led Clemson from underdog to dynasty, winning two national championships in 2016 and 2018 and reaching four playoff title games.
The wishbone offense, pioneered by Darrell Royal at Texas in 1968, dominated college football in the 1970s with its triple-option running attack.
The 'Power I' formation features a fullback and tailback aligned behind the quarterback and was the foundation of many college and NFL ground games.
Matt Leinart, Reggie Bush, and the 2005 USC Trojans were considered one of the most talented college teams ever before their title was later vacated.
The NFL's Guardian Caps — soft-shell helmet covers used in practice — were mandated in 2022 to reduce head impact severity during training camp.
Modern football gloves use sticky silicone grip surfaces that dramatically improve catching ability compared to bare hands.
The NFL banned the use of stickum — a tacky substance applied to hands and jerseys — after Lester Hayes of the Raiders abused it in 1981.
Rib protectors, or 'flak jackets,' became popular after quarterback injuries in the 1980s and are still worn by many skill-position players.
The 'Heisman House' advertising campaign by Nissan features past Heisman Trophy winners living together, becoming one of football's most beloved commercial series.
College football's 'Gameday' show on ESPN, hosted for years by Lee Corso, Kirk Herbstreit, and others, has become a Saturday morning tradition since 1987.
The NFL's Man of the Year award, renamed the Walter Payton Man of the Year Award in 1999, honors a player's community service and on-field excellence.
Peyton Manning and Eli Manning are the only brothers to each win a Super Bowl as starting quarterbacks.
Kurt Warner went from stocking shelves at a grocery store to winning NFL MVP and Super Bowl MVP, one of football's greatest rags-to-riches stories.
The 'Greatest Show on Turf' Rams offense averaged over 30 points per game for three consecutive seasons from 1999 to 2001.
Sam Darnold, Baker Mayfield, and Josh Allen were all drafted in the first round of the 2018 draft, but their career paths diverged dramatically.
The 2007 New York Giants defeated three number-one seeds on the road in the playoffs before upsetting the undefeated Patriots in Super Bowl XLII.
The concept of 'positionless football' has emerged in the 2020s, with players like Micah Parsons and Tyrann Mathieu lining up at multiple positions.
College football's 'transfer portal,' introduced in 2018, allows players to move between schools freely and has revolutionized roster management.
The 'option route' allows a receiver to read the defense mid-play and choose which direction to cut, requiring chemistry between quarterback and receiver.
NFL mouthguards have evolved from simple rubber pieces to custom-molded devices with embedded sensors that measure impact forces on the jaw.
The 'student section' at college football games often creates coordinated card displays, cell phone light shows, and chants unique to each university.
Clemson's tradition of running down the hill and touching Howard's Rock before home games at Memorial Stadium is one of college football's most dramatic entrances.
The San Diego Chargers relocated to Los Angeles in 2017, becoming the second NFL team to share a market with the Rams.
The 'Squib Kick' is a low, bouncing kickoff designed to eat clock and prevent a long return, often used at the end of halves.
Woody Hayes coached Ohio State for 28 seasons, winning five national championships and thirteen Big Ten titles before his infamous sideline incident ended his career.
The NFL's 'Hip Drop Tackle' was banned starting in 2024 after studies showed it caused a disproportionate number of lower-leg injuries.
Joe Theismann's career-ending leg injury on Monday Night Football in 1985, caused by Lawrence Taylor's tackle, remains one of the most replayed moments in football history.
The 'Spread Option' offense used by coaches like Rich Rodriguez and Urban Meyer revolutionized college football by combining quarterback running with spread passing concepts.
